--- title: "Load candidates into Slick" --- ```{=html}

SPRFMO

South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
```
Explore MP29 and MP32 Results in Slick
Slick is an interactive tool for exploring MSE results. The simulation is run in `jmMSE`; this repository converts the resulting performance table into a Slick file. ## What is needed The conversion can use either: - the table of performance results produced by `jmMSE`; or - a saved `jmMSE` results object that already contains those performance results. The results must include MP29 and MP32, the operating-model and stock names, simulation year and replicate, and the calculated performance measures. At a minimum, the results must include spawning biomass relative to $SB_{MSY}$, fishing mortality relative to $F_{MSY}$, and catch. Interannual catch change (IACC) is included when available. The current reference comparison has been completed for MP29 and MP32 with 100 simulation replicates through 2050. It is stored locally in `jmMSE` as: - `model/candidates/reference/runs.rds`: the complete simulation object; and - `model/candidates/reference/performance.rds`: the smaller table used for Slick. These are reference-operating-model results. They are suitable for checking the candidate calculations and comparing MP29 with MP32, but they are not a substitute for robustness testing across the full set of operating models. ## Run or extend the reference comparison From the `jmMSE` folder on branch `devel`: ```sh Rscript run_candidate_mps.R \ --candidates=tun29,tun32 \ --mode=reference \ --cores=1 ``` The candidate codes come from `candidate_mps.csv`. Additional hockeystick or powerramp trials can be included for contrast, for example: ```sh Rscript run_candidate_mps.R \ --candidates=tun25,tun29,tun32 \ --mode=reference \ --cores=1 ``` The runner uses the estimator and rule implementations saved with the original tuning runs, then applies the selected rows from `candidate_mps.csv`. This preserves the calculations used to tune the candidates. Moving to a newer combined-index implementation should be treated as a method change requiring the operating models to be rebuilt and the candidates to be retuned and revalidated. The validated reference run used R 4.6.1 with FLCore 2.6.30, FLFishery 0.4.0, mse 2.4.8.9004, msemodules 0.2.2, and FLjjm 0.3.4. The run folder contains the complete `sessionInfo.txt` record. ## Prepare the Slick file From the main `jmMSE26` folder: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} source("R/build_candidate_slick.R") slick <- build_candidate_slick( performance_file = "../jmMSE/model/candidates/reference/performance.rds", out_file = "output/jm_candidates.slick" ) ``` ::: The script organizes the results by: - management procedure (MP29 or MP32); - operating model and stock; - simulation replicate and year; and - performance measure, including Kobe-plot values for $SB/SB_{MSY}$ and $F/F_{MSY}$. Before saving the Slick file, the script checks that the expected dimensions and labels agree. The technical checks are shown in the code for analysts who maintain the export. The current file contains 100 simulations, one reference operating model, two management procedures, four performance measures, and 26 annual values from 2025 through 2050. ## Add robustness results later Once the derived robustness operating models have been rebuilt under an agreed and recorded package set, run the candidate script with `--mode=robustness` and pass its performance table to the same Slick conversion. Do not combine reference and robustness results produced under different package sets without first confirming that the candidate calculations are equivalent. ## Inspect or launch ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(Slick) slick <- readRDS("output/jm_candidates.slick") Check(slick) methods::validObject(slick) dim(Value(Timeseries(slick))) Metadata(MPs(slick)) Design(slick) App(slick = slick) ``` ::: ## What to save with the Slick file Save the Slick file together with the original performance results, their digital fingerprint, the exact saved versions of `jmMSE` and `jmMSE26`, and the versions of the R packages used.
